questionHtml: A planet has a radius of 3.1 × 106 m. At a point P a distance 2.4 × 107 m above the surface of the planet the gravitational field strength is 2.2 N kg–1. Calculate the gravitational potential at point P, include an appropriate unit for your answer.
markschemeHtml:
V = «–2.2 × (3.1 × 106 + 2.4 × 107) =» «–» 6.0 × 107 J kg–1

 

Unit is essential

Allow eg MJ kg–1 if power of 10 is correct

Allow other correct SI units eg m2s–2, N m kg–1

[1 mark]
